claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 10 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children. Let me start with a few questions.

**First, could you please tell me the names of the children you're claiming Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and wondering if I'm eligible.

I have one child - Alex. They're 10 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 10 years old.

**To continue, could you please tell me: Does Alex live with you as their main place of residence?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. They live with my ex-partner.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Alex lives with your ex-partner rather than with you, I need to understand this situation better.

**Could you tell me: Are you currently paying child maintenance or child support to your ex-partner for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do help out. I contribute money towards Alex's upkeep - I help with things like clothes and school expenses.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Before I can make a final determination, I need to check the specific rules about Child Benefit eligibility when a child lives with someone else. Let me review the relevant guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! Based on the information you've provided and the guidance I've just reviewed, I can now assess your eligibility.

According to the rules, when a child lives with someone else (in this case, your ex-partner), you can continue to receive Child Benefit beyond the initial 8-week period **if you contribute to the child's upkeep by at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment itself**.

You've mentioned that you do help with Alex's upkeep by contributing money towards clothes and school expenses. This is exactly the type of contribution that counts towards eligibility.

**However, I need to clarify one more important detail: Do your contributions to Alex's upkeep (for clothes, school expenses, etc.) equal or exceed the amount of the Child Benefit payment itself?** 

For context, the current Child Benefit rate is approximately £23.80 per week (or around £104 per month) for the first child. Your contributions need to be at least this amount regularly to maintain eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, to be honest, I contribute about £20 a week towards Alex's upkeep. Is that enough?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Unfortunately, based on the rules I've reviewed, your contributions of £20 per week fall slightly short of what's required.

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, to continue receiving Child Benefit for a child who lives with someone else, your contributions to the child's upkeep must be **at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment itself**.

The current Child Benefit rate for the first child is approximately £23.80 per week. Since you're contributing £20 per week, which is less than this amount, you would not meet the contribution threshold needed to qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.

You would typically only receive Child Benefit for the first 8 weeks after Alex went to live with your ex-partner, but after that period, you would not be eligible to continue receiving it unless your contributions increase to at least match the Child Benefit payment amount.

Now I have all the information I need to provide you with my formal assessment. Let me do that for you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

Based on my assessment, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

The key issue is that while you do contribute towards Alex's upkeep, your contributions of £20 per week are below the threshold required by the rules. You would need to contribute at least approximately £23.80 per week (the current Child Benefit rate) to maintain eligibility for a child who lives with someone else.

**However, it's worth noting:** If you were able to increase your contributions to meet or exceed the Child Benefit payment amount, you would then become eligible. I'd recommend contacting the Child Benefit Office directly if you think your circumstances might change or if you have any questions about your specific situation.

reasonings: Alex is 10 years old and does not live with the claimant - they live with the claimant's ex-partner. According to the Child Benefit rules, when a child lives with someone else, the original person can only continue to receive Child Benefit beyond the initial 8 weeks if they contribute to the child's upkeep by at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment itself. The claimant contributes £20 per week towards Alex's upkeep (clothes and school expenses), but the current Child Benefit rate for the first child is approximately £23.80 per week. Since the claimant's contributions (£20/week) are less than the required amount (£23.80/week), they do not meet the eligibility criteria for continued Child Benefit payments beyond the initial 8-week period.

The claimant's weekly contributions of £20 fall short of the approximately £23.80 weekly Child Benefit rate required for eligibility beyond the initial 8-week period."}
</invoke>
